Here is the user's input (remember to respond with a markdown code snippet of a json blob with a single action, and NOTHING else): How do I create experiences in the ZMP?
How do I create experiences in the ZMP?

Here is the user's input (remember to respond with a markdown code snippet of a json blob with a single action, and NOTHING else): How do I create experiences in the ZMP? AI: { "action": "ZetaKnowledgeBaseQA", "action_input": "How do I create experiences in the ZMP?" } Human: TOOL RESPONSE: --------------------- Answer: To create experiences in the ZMP, you can use the Experience Builder tool. This tool allows you to build, deploy, and analyze one-to-one experiences for customers. You can access the Experience Builder by clicking on Experiences > Builder from the taskbar on the left of the screen. Within the Experience Builder, you can use a combination of four types of nodes (Trigger Event, Event, Delay, Actions, and Split) to create experiences with just a few clicks. Additionally, you can create experiences based on Campaign Events, with additional filters such as skip, unsubscribe, bounce, and delivered. Experiences in the ZMP are evaluated one node at a time, with the logic created within an experience evaluated for the user only in the node they are currently passing through or being held at. Here is the user's input (remember to respond with a markdown code snippet of a json blob with a single action, and NOTHING else): How do I create Campaigns for Zeta? AI: ```json { "action": "ZetaKnowledgeBaseQA", "action_input": "How do I create Campaigns for Zeta?" } ``` Human: TOOL RESPONSE: --------------------- Answer: You can create campaigns for Zeta using the Zeta Marketing Platform. The ZMP Knowledge Base provides FAQs about Zeta Campaigns, including how to create folders, sharing access, custom font html, attaching documents, coupon codes, send triggers, seed list, tags, reports, archived campaigns, multi-version campaigns, seed recipients, Prime Time dashboard, default send time, custom KPIs, link names and tags, and HTML content. You can also create Triggered Campaigns, which are 1:1 event-based campaigns that cater to a single subscriber based on their past events or behavior. Additionally, you can integrate your data into the Zeta Marketing Platform with Adobe Campaign. For more detailed information on how to create campaigns, I recommend referring to the ZMP Knowledge Base and the specific documentation for the type of campaign you want to create. Here is the user's input (remember to respond with a markdown code snippet of a json blob with a single action, and NOTHING else): What is the best time to send an email? AI: ```json { "action": "ZetaKnowledgeBaseQA", "action_input": "What is the best time to send an email?" } ``` Human: TOOL RESPONSE: --------------------- The best time to send an email can vary depending on your audience and their engagement habits. Zeta Global's Prime Time Optimization feature can help optimize the send time of your campaign for each individual member in your audience to improve open rates. Initially, with a new audience, prime time is effectively random and spreads the send out pretty evenly throughout the send window. Over time, the platform will learn the engagement habits of individual audience members and will begin to focus on the following "hot spots" for each member: engagement is measured by an hour of the day and day of the week, and lift is measured against a 10% control group that the platform deploys immediately at the beginning of the send window. So, it's recommended to use Zeta's Prime Time Optimization feature to determine the best time to send your email. USER'S INPUT -------------------- Okay, now use the information above to help answer the question. ```json { "action": "Final Answer", "action_input": "The best time to send an email can vary depending on your audience and their engagement habits. Zeta Global's Prime Time Optimization feature can help optimize the send time of your campaign for each individual member in your audience to improve open rates. It measures engagement by hour of the day and day of the week, and lift is measured against a 10% control group that the platform deploys immediately at the beginning of the send window. Therefore, it is recommended to use Zeta's Prime Time Optimization feature to determine the best time to send your email." } ```

Zeta Global's Prime Time Optimization feature can help optimize the send time of your campaign for each individual member in your audience to improve open rates. Initially, with a new audience, prime time is effectively random and spreads the send out pretty evenly throughout the send window. Over time, the platform will learn the engagement habits of individual audience members and will begin to focus on the following "hot spots" for each member: engagement is measured by an hour of the day and day of the week, and lift is measured against a 10% control group that the platform deploys immediately at the beginning of the send window. So, it is recommended to use Zeta's Prime Time Optimization feature to determine the best time to send an email for your specific audience.
